content_markdown:
The graph shows how the current $I$ in a resistor varies with the voltage $V$ applied across it.

Which of the following gives the resistance of the resistor, when $I = I_1$?

diagram_metadata.description: A graph of Current I (y-axis) against Voltage V (x-axis). The curve starts at the origin (0,0) and curves downwards (decreasing gradient). A specific point is marked with coordinates (V1, I1).

options.label: B
options.text: The slope of the curve at the point $(V_1, I_1)$

options.label: D
options.text: The inverse of the slope of the curve at the point $(V_1, I_1)$

vibe_explanation: Resistance is defined by Ohm's Law as the ratio of potential difference to current at a specific point, $R = V/I$. While the slope of an I-V graph relates to conductance (or differential resistance if taking the derivative), the absolute resistance at any specific state is simply the ratio of the coordinates $V_1$ and $I_1$.
